summary: DHH announces the RubyConf 2005 agenda, highlighting three Rails-focused talks and a hands-on workshop. He expresses excitement about Rails having 'come of age' at this conference and encourages the Ruby and Rails community to attend. The post also highlights other talks he's looking forward to, including Jim Weirich on DSLs and the YARV progress report. It's a brief, enthusiastic promotional post for the San Diego event.
key_points: RubyConf 2005 features three Rails talks and a hands-on workshop, signaling Rails' growing prominence in the Ruby community
DHH frames this as the first RubyConf where Rails has 'come of age' with real-world adoption stories to share
The post highlights the broader Ruby ecosystem excitement beyond Rails, including DSLs and YARV
money_quotes: It's going to be a Rails bonanza!
This RubyConf is the first where Rails will have "come of age" and people will be swapping all those juicy real-life stories.
key_insight: By late 2005, Rails had matured enough to dominate the RubyConf agenda, marking its transition from upstart framework to established force in the Ruby community.
